critic_findings.message: fetch stub casts non-string url to `URL` but `fetch` also accepts `Request`; casting a Request to URL silently returns '[object Request]'. Low-severity since fetchJSON always passes a string today, but the cast is unsound.

findings_details.title: stop() leaves isRunning true via knownTerminals until next focus refresh
findings_details.body: stop() removes the key from sessionsRef but does not clear known.knownTerminals (React state). The OR fallback then returns true via the knownTerminals branch until visibilitychange/focus/pageshow triggers useKnownTerminals.refresh(). Pre-fix this was instant; this is a regression in user-stop UX.
findings_details.fix: Either call known.refresh() after stopSession resolves, or expose an imperative key-removal callback from useKnownTerminals and invoke it from stop().

findings_details.title: Override spread position is fragile under future refactors
findings_details.body: isRunning override depends on sitting after ...actions in the return object. A future spread or reorder silently breaks the fix with no TypeScript error.
findings_details.fix: Add a one-line comment marking the line as an intentional override (e.g. // Override: falls back to knownTerminals when no local instance — issue #155).

findings_details.title: MD013 disabled globally rather than scoped to prose directories
findings_details.body: Global suppression broader than needed. Stated motivation is docs/test-plans and CLAUDE.md.
findings_details.fix: Optional — scope with overrides[].globs targeting docs/**/*.md, README.md, CLAUDE.md.

findings_details.title: Fetch failure -> localStorage fallback now causes false-positive running badges
findings_details.body: Pre-fix, isRunning only checked sessionsRef so a 5xx during page load = clean empty badges. Post-fix, isRunning ORs in known.knownTerminals which on fetch failure is populated from knownMapFromStorage(). Every previously-known terminal now appears running on a 5xx blip until a successful fetch.
findings_details.fix: Track has-ever-succeeded since mount. On the FIRST fetch failure, leave knownTerminals empty (correct unknown state). Only fall back to localStorage on subsequent failures.

findings_details.title: Second test's waitFor(size === 0) is vacuously satisfied at mount
findings_details.body: useState(new Map()) initial value satisfies size === 0 immediately, before useEffect fires the fetch. The empty-fallback test could pass even if the fetch was broken.
findings_details.fix: Wait for the fetch mock to be called before asserting: await waitFor(() => expect(vi.mocked(fetch)).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.stringContaining('/api/terminals'), expect.anything())).

findings_details.title: Pre-existing: refreshInFlight guard drops concurrent refresh callers
findings_details.body: Pre-existing — not introduced by this PR. Flagging because the new override makes knownTerminals more user-visible.
findings_details.fix: Out of scope for this PR; track in a follow-up if observed during QA.

findings_details.title: Hard-coded line-number reference in comment will rot
findings_details.body: Comment cites useKnownTerminals.ts:93-95. Line numbers shift, leading future readers to wrong code.

findings_details.title: Missing vi.unstubAllGlobals() teardown in the new describe block
findings_details.body: vi.stubGlobal persists for the rest of the test file unless restored. Future tests added below this describe block would inherit a route-aware fetch stub.
findings_details.fix: Add afterEach(() => vi.unstubAllGlobals()) inside the new describe block.
